title: Agentic Coding's Xcode Implementation Guide (Part 2): Creating a Playable iOS Demo with a Single Prompt
description: From 0 to 1 in practice: Newtons Apple - Using Gemini CLI + Xcode MCP to generate interactive SwiftUI/SpriteKit physics projects and let the Agent fix compilation errors itself.
content:
**TL;DR:** Mastering the secrets to issuing precise R&D instructions to AI. As the second part of the practical guide, this article demonstrates how to use a structured Prompt consisting of **goals + constraints + acceptance criteria** to allow the Gemini CLI agent to directly generate an interactive physics gravity experiment app from scratch within Xcode. It also shares a practical approach to guide the agent to actively observe compilation errors and complete self-correction within a "closed loop."

## What you will gain from this article

If you have already completed the configuration in the previous article, this article will guide you through a complete workflow:

- Creating a new iOS project

- Issuing a goal-oriented Prompt via Gemini CLI

- Allowing the agent to iteratively generate code and fix build issues

- Verifying the interaction in Preview/Simulator

The core of this article is not "copying code," but learning a **reusable collaboration pattern**.

Series Navigation:

- [Part 1: From Concept to Runnable Environment](/posts/agentic-coding-xcode-gemini-01-overview/)

- Part 2 (This Article): From Hints to a Runnable Demo

- [Part 3: Troubleshooting Checklist, Compatibility, and Team Collaboration](/posts/agentic-coding-xcode-gemini-03-troubleshooting/)

## Step 1: Create a Blank SwiftUI Project

In Xcode:

1.`File > New > Project...`2. Select`iOS > App`3. Enter the project name (e.g., ...)`NewtonsApple`4. After saving, ensure the code can be compiled and run.

Then, execute the following command in the project root directory:

```bash
gemini mcp add xcode-tools xcrun mcpbridge
gemini
```Accept the permission request from Xcode.

## Step 2: Give the Agent a "verifiable" goal

It's recommended to use the "goal + constraint + acceptance" structure below, instead of simply saying "Make me an animation."

![Newton's Apple Demo Result](https://peterfriese.dev/_astro/newtons-apple.BNoUnKp9_Zm3pe1.webp)

_Image: The final interactive effect we want to build (Source: peterfriese.dev)_

Usable prompts (Chinese rewritten version):

```text
请将当前默认 SwiftUI 首页替换为 SpriteKit 场景并嵌入 SwiftUI。

目标：构建一个物理小实验。
1) 屏幕中央显示“Hello World”，并作为静态刚体。
2) 点按屏幕时，从顶部随机生成水果 emoji（🍎🍌🍇），让它们受重力下落并与文字碰撞。
3) 底部添加一个“Antigravity”按钮，按住时反转重力，松开后恢复。

约束：
- 代码拆分清晰，避免把所有逻辑塞进一个 View。
- 先保证编译通过，再优化细节。
- 每次改动后主动构建并修复报错，直到能运行。

验收标准：
- 能在 Preview 或 Simulator 中交互。
- 点按可连续生成 emoji。
- Antigravity 按住时可见明显反重力效果。
```## Step 3: Make the Agent perform a "closed loop" instead of a "one-time generation"

You can drive the session with the following rhythm:

1. **Generate the minimum implementation first**

2. **Build immediately** (let the Agent read errors)

3. **Fix errors** (fix only one type of problem at a time)

4. **Build again**

5. **Finally, fine-tune the experience** (collision parameters, button feedback, etc.)

This is much more stable than "directly demanding the final perfect version".

## My commonly used follow-up question template

After the first generation, don't end immediately. Continue asking follow-up questions:

```text
请先执行构建检查。
如果失败：列出前 3 个关键错误，按优先级逐个修复；
每修复一次就重新构建，直到通过。
```Then add the user experience layer:

```text
现在做两件事：
1) 调整水果碰撞与反弹参数，让视觉更自然；
2) 给 Antigravity 按钮加按压态反馈（例如透明度或缩放）。
```## Why does this approach work?

Because we break the task down into three phases:

$$
\text{Generate} \rightarrow \text{Build/Observe} \rightarrow \text{Fix/Refine}

$$
This significantly reduces the "chain reaction" errors introduced by a large model's one-time output.

## Common Failure Signals (and What to Do)

### 1) The code looks long, but I can't seem to get it to work.

Solution: Narrow down the convergence range.

```text
先暂停新增功能，只保留最小可运行版本。
仅修复当前编译错误，不做重构。
```### 2) The program runs, but the interaction is strange.

Solution: Change it to "Observable parameter tuning request".

```text
请把与物理参数相关的常量集中到一个结构体，并标注用途。
然后给出推荐参数范围与默认值。
```### 3) Agent changes are too aggressive.

Solution: Limit the boundaries of changes.

```text
只允许修改 Scene 层与按钮组件，不要改 App 入口和工程配置。
```## A Golden Rule for Everyday Use

Upgrade Prompts from "Command Statements" to "Engineering Task Lists":

- Define Clear Goals

- Define Clear Constraints

- Define Clear Acceptance Processes

- Define Clear Iteration Strategy

This way, the Agent will act like a "teammate who can execute," rather than a "chatbot that randomly provides answers."

## Summary

By now, you've mastered the most crucial practical actions in Agentic Coding:

- Describe the initiating task with high-quality goals

Enable the Agent to proactively experience the build-repair loop

- Reduce irrelevant changes through boundary constraints

The next article will specifically discuss "pitfalls": version compatibility, authorization pop-ups, MCP response formats, and implementation suggestions for CI/CD and standards within the team.
